page: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU runs at 2.4 GHz and includes a 320 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) for accelerated matrix multiplication and vector operations.

The hardware configuration includes 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8 GB of RAM per physical core. For storage-heavy workloads, the instance is equipped with 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps. This dense combination of high-capacity local storage and fast DDR5 memory makes the instance highly resource-dense, offering strong qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments that require high local throughput without relying on external network storage.

Benchmark results highlight the server's capabilities, particularly in database and memory-bound operations. The instance achieved top-tier performance in Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput, alongside strong multi-core CPU execution in stress-ng and Passmark tests. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong read and write speeds, reaching 5004.08 GB/sec for cached small blocks. It also demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations and multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression. This profile makes the i7i.16xlarge highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, NoSQL databases, real-time analytics, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. It is equipped with 512 GB of DDR5 memory and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The CPU architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for specialized mathematical operations. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving throughput, and image processing, alongside strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth. This instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for storage-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, data analytics, and high-throughput web serving applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15,000 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15,000 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-capacity storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and massive local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
15,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
512 GB of fast DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and static web serving
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for large-scale databases, real-time analytics, and high-throughput web serving
